outcomes.statement: a user can log in via an external SSO identity provider
a logged-in user stays logged in across requests without re-authenticating each time
a logged-in user can log out and their session ends

requirements.description: register the app with the SSO identity provider and configure client id/secret, issuer, scopes, and the allowed redirect/callback URL
a persisted user/identity record (db schema) keyed by the provider subject, created on first successful login
an initiate-login route that redirects the browser to the provider authorize endpoint with state + nonce
the redirect/callback route that exchanges the authorization code for tokens, validates state/nonce, and establishes the session
validate the ID token signature, issuer, audience, and expiry against the provider's keys before trusting any claim
a server-side session store that issues a session on login and can look one up on each request
middleware that reads the session cookie on each request and attaches the authenticated user, so users stay logged in
refresh the access/ID token using the refresh token before expiry so the session does not silently break
a logout route that destroys the server session, clears the cookie, and optionally hits the provider end-session endpoint
on callback, link the provider identity to an existing user (or create one) so a returning user maps to one stable account
CSRF/state protection on the auth flow: an unguessable state param issued at login and verified at callback
the client secret and signing/session keys are loaded from configuration/secrets, never hard-coded or committed
user-facing handling for the auth failure paths: provider error, denied consent, state mismatch, expired/invalid token, callback without a pending login

concerns.reason: the plan states SSO is the only way in; building a local password path would be scope drift and add password-storage liability the plan explicitly avoids

mustHaves.statement: a user can log in via the external SSO provider
a logged-in user stays logged in across requests
a user can log out and the session ends

requiredEdges.why: the login redirect needs the provider's authorize URL, client id, and scopes
the code-for-token exchange needs the client id/secret and token endpoint
the callback completes the flow the login route started (and verifies the state it issued)
the callback must validate the returned ID token before establishing a session
the callback verifies the state/nonce issued at login to prevent CSRF on the auth flow
after token validation the callback links/creates the user account
a successful callback establishes a session in the store
linking/creating a user writes to the user table
a session references the persisted user it belongs to
middleware looks the session up in the store on each request
refresh updates the tokens held in the session
a refreshed token must be re-validated before it is trusted
logout destroys the session in the store
validation needs the provider's issuer, audience, and signing-key (JWKS) endpoint
provider config loads the client secret from managed secrets, not source
the login route issues the unguessable state/nonce the callback later verifies
the auth error states are the failure branches of the callback flow
